questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the acute angles formed measures 73°. What is the measure of the corresponding angle on the opposite side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal cutting parallel lines are congruent, so the angle is also 73°.

questions.question: In right triangle DEF, angle D = 90°, DE = 5, and DF = 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse EF?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean theorem: EF² = 5² + 12² = 25 + 144 = 169, so EF = 13.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a vertex angle of 44°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. Let each base angle be x. Then 44° + x + x = 180°, so 2x = 136°, and x = 68°.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at vertex Q measures 125°. If angle P measures 51°, what is the measure of angle R?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 125° = 51° + angle R, giving angle R = 74°.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 7, 9, and x. If the triangle must satisfy the triangle inequality, what is the range of possible integer values for x?
questions.explanation: The triangle inequality requires: 7 + 9 > x, 7 + x > 9, and 9 + x > 7. This gives x < 16, x > 2, and x > −2. So 2 < x < 16; for integers: x ∈ {3, 4, ..., 15}.
